description: Merge multiple rblocks into a single rigid body.

python_sdk_alternative.workaround: Use itasca.command('rblock merge ...') via command interface

keywords.name: density
keywords.description: Rigid block density with default value 1.0.

keywords.name: group
keywords.description: The group name at slot slot is set to s for the newly created rigid blocks. If slot is not specified, then the group name at slot Default is set to s .

keywords.name: id
keywords.description: The ID of the newly created rigid block is set to i .

keywords.name: keep-source
keywords.description: The rigid blocks used as the source of the merged rigid block are retained.

keywords.name: rounding
keywords.description: Introduce rigid block rounding. Rigid blocks are represented by a core shape that is composed of {linear facets in 2D; triangular facets in 3D}. The core shape is convex, closed and manifold. When rounding is nonzero, the core shape is expanded in a directions by a {circle in 2D; sphere in 3D} of a specified radius with center passing along all points on the exterior of the core shape. The value of the rounding corresponds to the radius of this expansion {circle in 2D; sphere in 3D}. If the relative keyword is given, the rounding is computed as the product of f and the radius of a {circle in 2D; sphere in 3D} with the same size as the rigid block. When the rounding is changed so are the inertial properties.
